data_in.description: MoNA EI library in MSP format (GC-MS Spectra)

data_out.description: Reorganized MoNA EI library object with SMILES field properly populated from Comment field

domain_knowledge: MoNA EI library files encode SMILES information in the Comment field rather than a dedicated SMILES field, requiring field relocation for MS-DIAL compatibility.
mspcompiler's reorganize_mona function performs bidirectional field mapping: extracting structured chemical identifiers from unstructured comment text and populating the canonical SMILES field.
The MSP format is a line-delimited text standard for mass spectral metadata; field order and naming are critical for downstream software recognition.
MS-DIAL and mspcompiler expect SMILES and InChIKey in dedicated metadata fields; Comment field content is ignored during library matching operations.

direct_checks: verify file exists: input MoNA EI library file (e.g., MoNA download .msp or .json format) from https://mona.fiehnlab.ucdavis.edu/downloads
script_runs: reorganize_mona() function executes without error on valid MoNA input file
file_format_is: output object is an R list (or equivalent serialized structure)
field_present: output object contains all required fields expected by downstream mspcompiler pipeline steps (exact field names and structure require expert review of source code)
row_count_equals: output object row/record count matches or correctly summarizes input MoNA library record count
output_matches_reference: if mspcompiler repository includes test fixtures or example outputs, verify reorganize_mona() output structure and content match reference deposit or example in repo
expert_review: verify that the internal list format produced by reorganize_mona() conforms to the schema expected by downstream pipeline functions (read_lib, assign_smiles, separate_polarity, etc.)

verify that field mapping from MoNA source format to internal format is complete and semantically correct (e.g., metadata fields, spectral peak data, compound identifiers are preserved or correctly transformed)
verify that no spectral information or compound metadata is lost or corrupted during reorganization

cmd: library(mspcompiler); mona_raw <- read_lib('{inputs.mona_ei_raw_msp}'); mona_reorganized <- reorganize_mona(mona_raw); saveRDS(mona_reorganized, 'mona_reorganized.rds'); cat('Reorganization complete. Input rows:', nrow(mona_raw), 'Output rows:', nrow(mona_reorganized), 'SMILES populated:', sum(!is.na(mona_reorganized$SMILES)), '\n')

task_description: Apply the reorganize_mona function to a downloaded MoNA EI library MSP file to extract SMILES information from the Comment field and restructure it into the internal mspcompiler list format, producing a properly formatted library object ready for downstream pipeline steps.

task_inputs.label: MoNA EI library MSP file (GC-MS Spectra)

task_objective: Implement the reorganize_mona function to convert a MoNA EI library file into the internal list format, with validation of required fields and output row count.

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: MoNA, Reorganized MoNA EI library object with SMILES field properly populated from Comment field.
workflow_description: 1. Load the MoNA EI library MSP file using read_lib() with type='EI' to parse the initial msp structure into an R list object. 2. Apply reorganize_mona() function to extract SMILES information from the Comment field and populate the SMILES field in the library object. 3. Verify the output object contains the expected field structure (SMILES field populated) and that row count matches the input.
